+## Session Notes (2026-06-10, memo promotion)
+The memo was renamed and revised to v0.2 "vision": it now names the
+one-grammar/three-consumers spine (codec, validator, recension), declares the
+recension scope boundary (canon-side rides the Diptych lexer; cross-universe renames
+ride the zipper registries), and holds the removal-conditions ledger of tolerated
+inconsistencies awaiting this machinery. Heat-shape consequence: this heat remains
+the format/codec prototype; the validator intent flowers in VOS (₣AD territory);
+recension is unassigned to any heat yet.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# jjezs_paddock ₣AZ

# Paddock: cmk-diptych-prototype

## Context

Diptych is a dual-representation document format for concept models, replacing AsciiDoc
with a format natively optimized for two consumers: git (recto: word-per-line) and
LLMs (verso: joined prose). Prefix characters replace paired delimiters. The mapping
spine is preserved; AsciiDoc ceremony is eliminated.

Key concepts: canon (authoritative source), lectionary (selection rules),
lectio (task-focused extract for constrained agents), recto/verso (git/LLM faces),
codec (trivial mechanical transform between them).

## References

- `Memos/memo-20260209-diptych-format-study.md` — Discovery draft v0.1 (2026-02-09)
- `Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc` — Semantic structure Diptych replaces the expression of
- `Tools/cmk/AXMCM-ClaudeMarkConceptMemo.md` — Earlier exploration, superseded by Diptych
- ₣Aj `rbk-axla-term-voicing-scrub` — Adjacent: redesigning voicing semantics (procedure collapse, entity affiliation). Diptych changes the syntax those voicings are expressed in.

## Session Notes (2026-02-25, axla-procedure-repair chat)

Connection identified: ₣Aj designs what voicings MEAN (collapse procedure hierarchy,
entity affiliation as hierarchy marker, drop language dimension). ₣AZ designs how
voicings are WRITTEN (prefix characters, no AsciiDoc ceremony). In Diptych, cheap
annotations liberate the voicing design — multiple single-concern annotation lines
instead of overloaded lineage chains.

jjx_curry: committed 9938ca0e

Done across three commits:

- `4e5cbc707` — pure rename: `memo-20260209-diptych-vision.md`, history-clean.
- `b79dbadf9` — the v0.2 revision: Spine section (one grammar, three consumers, zipper precedent), recension scope boundary, removal-conditions ledger holding our four tolerated debts, Recension in the liturgical vocabulary (marked *candidate, not yet cinched*), introspection memo cross-cited as validator feedstock, and phases restructured 5→7 — lexer joins codec, Validator and Recension get their own phases, migration moves last and is gated on round-trip fidelity.
- `9938ca0e` — ₣AZ paddock: stale reference repaired to the new filename, plus a shape note that ₣AZ stays the format/codec prototype, validator flowers in VOS/₣AD territory, recension is unassigned. While in there I demoted the body's bare `# Paddock:` H1 (the documented wire-format trap) and pruned two silks strings per the editing rule.

The retired heat's historical reference to the old filename was left stale deliberately — it records what the memo was called then.

**List state:** items 1, 2, 4, 5, 6 closed; the memo and its cross-link landed beyond the original list. The one survivor is **item 3** — the sub-letter discipline revision — which is now also the *right* survivor: it touches CLAUDE.md, which you've said is too soon to disturb, and the vision memo now holds everything a future session needs to execute it (the guidance, the rule set, the ledger). ₣Bd carries eight commits and tells the story.

aiTitle: Review specification system documents and annotations

message.content: Excellent, thank you.  Now: the direction I thought we would take before my open question to you was to start an audit process of adoc subdocuments versus their bash, and work on making clearer distinctions between the two.  I think we made a memo or maybe some heat paces (the 'scout' tool could help locate) about properly teasing apart what belongs in spec and what belongs in code.  Actually, there is an ACG document that may be the guidance I want to improve.  I want to increaase code quality by pulling comments out that belong in the specs, and increase spec quality by draining flowery language out, but I think we need a stronger statement of principle here to guide that.  And of course, I want to deeply enable LLMs to work well with this codebase, so your insight about guidance is keen.  I really want to avoid too much 'duplicate code': variations of the same idea become 'word cancers' if not prevented.

content.content:
1	<!--
2	Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	All rights reserved.
4	SPDX-License-Identifier: LicenseRef-Proprietary
5	-->
6	
7	# Allocation Coding Guide (ACG) — Reference the Home
8	
9	## Purpose
10	
11	ACG codifies one discipline: **don't recreate inline what already has a named
12	home — reference the home.** It is the language-neutral, allocation-facing
13	sibling to BCG (host bash), RCG (host Rust), WSG (Windows transport), and CBG
14	(cloud step). Those govern *how* code in one environment is written; ACG governs
15	*where a fact or a name is allowed to live*, and what source must do when a home
16	already exists: point at it, never restate it.
17	
18	ACG is the **source-side complement to MCM**. MCM builds the named homes — quoins
19	in specs, constants in code; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather
20	than recreate them. That complement is why ACG lives in the CMK kit beside MCM
21	and AXLA rather than in any single-language kit.
22	
23	ACG is **v1**: it blesses and names emergent practice and states the spine. It
24	does **not** mandate universals ("every verb must announce"); those are
25	candidates, confirmed move-by-move, not law. There is no qualify-time enforcement
26	in v1. Every move-type below is licensed precisely — most are detect-and-report,
27	not repair.
28	
29	## How this document is organized — frame, then catalog
30	
31	ACG separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form differently:
32	
33	- **The Frame** (prose, no IDs) — the spine, the three homes, the v1 posture, the
34	  move discipline. Systematic and interderivable; internalized, not cited by
35	  number.
36	- **The Move Catalog** (numbered `ACGm_`) — discrete, verifier-backed move-types,
37	  each of which something *points at*: a constant's breadcrumb comment, a
38	  detect-and-report verb, a conformance-engine row, a review flagging a
39	  violation. An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.
40	
41	This split is itself the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy): catalog the
42	discrete moves; systematize the discipline behind them.
43	
44	## The spine — reference the home
45	
46	The governing idea, stated once:
47	
48	> Don't recreate inline what has a named home. **Values → constants; concepts →
49	> quoin-refs.** MCM builds the homes; ACG governs source's duty to reference
50	> rather than recreate.
51	
52	This is the **allocation instance of Load-Bearing Complexity** (CLAUDE.md). A
53	recreated value or name is a non-load-bearing duplicate: its existence adds drift
54	risk without adding correctness, and the drift it invites is exactly the gap
55	between intent and behavior the principle forbids. BCG's and RCG's Constant
56	Discipline and Interface Contamination are the per-language instances of this;
57	ACG is the general statement those instantiate, plus the one thing a
58	single-language guide cannot state — *where prose with no code-home actually
59	goes.*
60	
61	## The three homes — allocate by when-read
62	
63	A fact has exactly one right home, fixed by *when it is read*:
64	
65	| When read | Home | Carries |
66	|-----------|------|---------|
67	| **Design-time** | the spec (MCM quoin) | what a thing *is* and why it is shaped so — the conceptual model |
68	| **Edit-time** | the source comment | operational mechanics only — language idiom, and the Palisade (foreign-boundary) characterization a maintainer needs *at the code* |
69	| **Execution-time** | the runtime announcement | what is happening now — intent printed as the operation runs |
70	
71	Most conceptual comments are **temporal misallocation**: design-time or
72	execution-time knowledge dumped into the edit-time medium, where it rots for lack
73	of a forcing function. The spec is re-read when the design is questioned; the
74	announcement is re-read every run; an edit-time comment restating either is read
75	by no one with a reason to correct it when it drifts.
76	
77	Two edit-time/execution-time source-doc forms are distinct and **both blessed**:
78	
79	- **Contract header** — a bounded edit-time comment stating a function's input
80	  contract and invariants. Read when editing the function.
81	- **Intent announcement** — an execution-time printout naming what the operation
82	  is doing. Read when running it.
83	
84	Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-parameter-shows-the-options
85	practice, live in `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil`) is blessed alongside happy-path
86	announcement: an error that lists the valid forms is an execution-time
87	announcement of the contract, not contamination.
88	
89	**The execution-time home is plural off the terminal.** "Announcement" reads as
90	a single printed stream because a CLI gives you one. A routine whose product
91	does not go to a terminal — an MCP command, a library call, a daemon — splits
92	it: the **product** (the value returned to the caller), the **trace**
93	(diagnostic breadcrumbs), and the **error** travel different media, bound per
94	project. Naming the full output-role roster is future work; one invariant earns
95	its place now: **trace has no re-read-every-run forcing function, so it rots like
96	an edit-time comment — never make it the sole home of design-time knowledge.**
97	
98	## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules
99	
100	Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:
101	
102	> **Mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply caught; everywhere else the verb
103	> is detect-and-report, never repair.**
104	
105	Code-side moves — the value resolves through a named constant, and shellcheck
106	plus the fast suite verify it — may mutate now. Spec- and document-side moves are
107	detect-and-report until the lexer/linter that would catch a wrong move exists.
108	
109	Three rules ride that litmus:
110	
111	1. **Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought.** Exclude the
112	   false-positive class first; declare the authoritative side before any
113	   alignment; gate scope to settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still
114	   moving. A sweep run against vocabulary two heats are still relocating only
115	   re-creates the drift it meant to kill.
116	2. **A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is
117	   trusted.** The conformance fixture (next section) is the canonical home for
118	   such checks — ACG eating its own dog food: a named home, referenced not
119	   recreated.
120	3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
121	   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
122	   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
123	   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.
124	
125	## The named home — the conformance fixture
126	
127	Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
128	**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
129	(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
130	is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
131	gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.
132	
133	The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
134	`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
135	awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
136	identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
137	`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
138	proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
139	(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.
140	
141	**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population
142	is deliberately deferred — each term gains its row behind its own cutover, owned
143	by the heat retiring it — never against vocabulary a hot heat is still moving
144	(move discipline, rule 1).
145	
146	## The Move Catalog
147	
148	Each move-type states five things: **Detect** (the rule that finds a site),
149	**Authority** (the ground truth a site is judged against), **Licensing**,
150	**Verifier**, and a countable **Done**. Headers tag the licensing: **🔧
151	mutate-now** (a wrong move is cheaply caught — repair in place) or **🔍
152	detect-only** (read and report; no repair until a verifier exists). Numbered from
153	101 to leave room for insertion; once a move has a citer it is never renumbered.
154	
155	### 🔧 ACGm_101: magic-string → constant
156	
157	- **Detect:** a literal value — a path fragment, a filename, a repeated number or
158	  string — that *constructs or addresses* something, where a named home exists or
159	  should. Not human-facing prose.
160	- **Authority:** the value resolves through a single named constant home (e.g.
161	  the `RBCC_rbr*_file` family in `rbcc_Constants.sh`). The constant is ground
162	  truth; every literal is a copy that can drift.
163	- **Licensing:** mutate-now — the constant either resolves or the verifier fails,
164	  so a wrong move dies loud and immediately.
165	- **Verifier:** bash — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` + `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`
166	  green; Rust — `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
167	- **Done:** every genuine construction site resolves through the constant; the
168	  constant carries a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
169	- **Triage:** naming a file *for a human* in a message is not a magic string —
170	  `doc_params`, `buc_info`/`buh_line` messages, and comments keep the literal.
171	  Exclude that class before sweeping.
172	- **Worked example:** the `rbrv.env` filename sweep is the first worked
173	  application of this move.
174	
175	### 🔍 ACGm_102: name-identity
176	
177	- **Detect:** an operation quoin (voiced `//axvo_procedure` or `//axvo_method`)
178	  whose display-text and implementing symbol disagree.
179	- **Authority:** the implementing symbol is the link (move discipline, rule 3) —
180	  a procedure's function name equals its quoin's display-text; a source-to-spec
181	  comment is the exception where no single symbol can carry it.
182	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side; no mutation until the lexer/linter
183	  exists.
184	- **Verifier:** none mechanized in v1 — read and reported by hand. The
185	  conformance fixture is the future verifier.
186	- **Done:** a report of operation-quoin / symbol disagreements; nothing mutated.
187	- **Formal dependency:** full mechanical decidability waits on an AXLA arity
188	  change — an explicit symbol lookahead on `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method`
189	  (one `mcm_inlay`, the prefix-disciplined source symbol; mirroring
190	  `axvo_tabtarget`'s literal lookahead). That change carries a migration across
191	  every live operation voicing, so it is deferred to its own pace (see *The
192	  AXLA/MCM interface*). Until it lands, the move is read against the existing
193	  `axvo_*` voicings — which already mark *which* quoins are operations — plus the
194	  implementation symbol named at an operation's detail site (`axhems_scoped_method`).
195	
196	### 🔍 ACGm_103: typed-parameter lookahead
197	
198	- **Detect:** a parameter whose type is resolvable by bounded lookahead but is
199	  not yet carried as a type quoin-ref.
200	- **Authority:** the lookahead scanning rule (below) — the parameter's type is
201	  the attribute-reference the marker reads.
202	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side.
203	- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter.
204	- **Done:** a report of parameters whose type is derivable but unhomed; nothing
205	  mutated.
206	
207	## The AXLA/MCM interface
208	
209	### The lookahead scanning rule
210	
211	When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
212	**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
213	terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
214	`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
215	distinct token type and does not shift that count.
216	
217	### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)
218	
219	ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
220	implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
221	lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
222	(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
223	`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
224	*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
225	mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
226	rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
227	dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.
228	
229	## Related Guides
230	
231	- **BCG** — host bash. Constant Discipline and Interface Contamination are the
232	  bash instances of the spine.
233	- **RCG** — host Rust. Constant Discipline (Beyond Strings), String Boundary
234	  Discipline, and Interface Contamination are the Rust instances.
235	- **WSG**, **CBG** — foreign-environment siblings (Windows transport, cloud step).
236	- **MCM** — builds the named homes ACG references; the design-time home.
237	- **AXLA** — the motif vocabulary; the lookahead scanning rule and the
238	  name-identity symbol-link dependency live against it.
239	- **CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" (mint)** — governs the naming homes
240	  themselves.
241	
242	## Acronym Registry
243	
244	| Term | Expansion |
245	|------|-----------|
246	| ACG | Allocation Coding Guide (this document) |
247	| The spine | "Reference the home" — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs |
248	| Home | The one right location for a fact, fixed by when it is read (design / edit / execution time) |
249	| Move-type | A catalogued `ACGm_` cleanup move: detect-rule, authority, licensing, verifier, countable Done |
250	| mutate-now | Licensing: a wrong move is cheaply caught, so the verb may repair in place |
251	| detect-only | Licensing: the verb reads and reports; no repair until a verifier exists |

<!--

All rights reserved.
SPDX-License-Identifier: LicenseRef-Proprietary
-->

# Allocation Coding Guide (ACG) — Reference the Home

## Purpose

ACG codifies one discipline: **don't recreate inline what already has a named
home — reference the home.** It is the language-neutral, allocation-facing
sibling to BCG (host bash), RCG (host Rust), WSG (Windows transport), and CBG
(cloud step). Those govern *how* code in one environment is written; ACG governs
*where a fact or a name is allowed to live*, and what source must do when a home
already exists: point at it, never restate it.

ACG is the **source-side complement to MCM**. MCM builds the named homes — quoins
in specs, constants in code; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather
than recreate them. That complement is why ACG lives in the CMK kit beside MCM
and AXLA rather than in any single-language kit.

ACG is **v1**: it blesses and names emergent practice and states the spine. It
does **not** mandate universals ("every verb must announce"); those are
candidates, confirmed move-by-move, not law. There is no qualify-time enforcement
in v1. Every move-type below is licensed precisely — most are detect-and-report,
not repair.

## How this document is organized — frame, then catalog

ACG separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form differently:

- **The Frame** (prose, no IDs) — the spine, the three homes, the v1 posture, the
  move discipline. Systematic and interderivable; internalized, not cited by
  number.
- **The Move Catalog** (numbered `ACGm_`) — discrete, verifier-backed move-types,
  each of which something *points at*: a constant's breadcrumb comment, a
  detect-and-report verb, a conformance-engine row, a review flagging a
  violation. An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.

This split is itself the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy): catalog the
discrete moves; systematize the discipline behind them.

## The spine — reference the home

The governing idea, stated once:

> Don't recreate inline what has a named home. **Values → constants; concepts →
> quoin-refs.** MCM builds the homes; ACG governs source's duty to reference
> rather than recreate.

This is the **allocation instance of Load-Bearing Complexity** (CLAUDE.md). A
recreated value or name is a non-load-bearing duplicate: its existence adds drift
risk without adding correctness, and the drift it invites is exactly the gap
between intent and behavior the principle forbids. BCG's and RCG's Constant
Discipline and Interface Contamination are the per-language instances of this;
ACG is the general statement those instantiate, plus the one thing a
single-language guide cannot state — *where prose with no code-home actually
goes.*

## The three homes — allocate by when-read

A fact has exactly one right home, fixed by *when it is read*:

| When read | Home | Carries |
|-----------|------|---------|
| **Design-time** | the spec (MCM quoin) | what a thing *is* and why it is shaped so — the conceptual model |
| **Edit-time** | the source comment | operational mechanics only — language idiom, and the Palisade (foreign-boundary) characterization a maintainer needs *at the code* |
| **Execution-time** | the runtime announcement | what is happening now — intent printed as the operation runs |

Most conceptual comments are **temporal misallocation**: design-time or
execution-time knowledge dumped into the edit-time medium, where it rots for lack
of a forcing function. The spec is re-read when the design is questioned; the
announcement is re-read every run; an edit-time comment restating either is read
by no one with a reason to correct it when it drifts.

Two edit-time/execution-time source-doc forms are distinct and **both blessed**:

- **Contract header** — a bounded edit-time comment stating a function's input
  contract and invariants. Read when editing the function.
- **Intent announcement** — an execution-time printout naming what the operation
  is doing. Read when running it.

Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-parameter-shows-the-options
practice, live in `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil`) is blessed alongside happy-path
announcement: an error that lists the valid forms is an execution-time
announcement of the contract, not contamination.

**The execution-time home is plural off the terminal.** "Announcement" reads as
a single printed stream because a CLI gives you one. A routine whose product
does not go to a terminal — an MCP command, a library call, a daemon — splits
it: the **product** (the value returned to the caller), the **trace**
(diagnostic breadcrumbs), and the **error** travel different media, bound per
project. Naming the full output-role roster is future work; one invariant earns
its place now: **trace has no re-read-every-run forcing function, so it rots like
an edit-time comment — never make it the sole home of design-time knowledge.**

## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules

Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:

> **Mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply caught; everywhere else the verb
> is detect-and-report, never repair.**

Code-side moves — the value resolves through a named constant, and shellcheck
plus the fast suite verify it — may mutate now. Spec- and document-side moves are
detect-and-report until the lexer/linter that would catch a wrong move exists.

Three rules ride that litmus:

1. **Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought.** Exclude the
   false-positive class first; declare the authoritative side before any
   alignment; gate scope to settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still
   moving. A sweep run against vocabulary two heats are still relocating only
   re-creates the drift it meant to kill.
2. **A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is
   trusted.** The conformance fixture (next section) is the canonical home for
   such checks — ACG eating its own dog food: a named home, referenced not
   recreated.
3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.

## The named home — the conformance fixture

Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.

The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.

**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population
is deliberately deferred — each term gains its row behind its own cutover, owned
by the heat retiring it — never against vocabulary a hot heat is still moving
(move discipline, rule 1).

## The Move Catalog

Each move-type states five things: **Detect** (the rule that finds a site),
**Authority** (the ground truth a site is judged against), **Licensing**,
**Verifier**, and a countable **Done**. Headers tag the licensing: **🔧
mutate-now** (a wrong move is cheaply caught — repair in place) or **🔍
detect-only** (read and report; no repair until a verifier exists). Numbered from
101 to leave room for insertion; once a move has a citer it is never renumbered.

### 🔧 ACGm_101: magic-string → constant

- **Detect:** a literal value — a path fragment, a filename, a repeated number or
  string — that *constructs or addresses* something, where a named home exists or
  should. Not human-facing prose.
- **Authority:** the value resolves through a single named constant home (e.g.
  the `RBCC_rbr*_file` family in `rbcc_Constants.sh`). The constant is ground
  truth; every literal is a copy that can drift.
- **Licensing:** mutate-now — the constant either resolves or the verifier fails,
  so a wrong move dies loud and immediately.
- **Verifier:** bash — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` + `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`
  green; Rust — `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
- **Done:** every genuine construction site resolves through the constant; the
  constant carries a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
- **Triage:** naming a file *for a human* in a message is not a magic string —
  `doc_params`, `buc_info`/`buh_line` messages, and comments keep the literal.
  Exclude that class before sweeping.
- **Worked example:** the `rbrv.env` filename sweep is the first worked
  application of this move.

### 🔍 ACGm_102: name-identity

- **Detect:** an operation quoin (voiced `//axvo_procedure` or `//axvo_method`)
  whose display-text and implementing symbol disagree.
- **Authority:** the implementing symbol is the link (move discipline, rule 3) —
  a procedure's function name equals its quoin's display-text; a source-to-spec
  comment is the exception where no single symbol can carry it.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side; no mutation until the lexer/linter
  exists.
- **Verifier:** none mechanized in v1 — read and reported by hand. The
  conformance fixture is the future verifier.
- **Done:** a report of operation-quoin / symbol disagreements; nothing mutated.
- **Formal dependency:** full mechanical decidability waits on an AXLA arity
  change — an explicit symbol lookahead on `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method`
  (one `mcm_inlay`, the prefix-disciplined source symbol; mirroring
  `axvo_tabtarget`'s literal lookahead). That change carries a migration across
  every live operation voicing, so it is deferred to its own pace (see *The
  AXLA/MCM interface*). Until it lands, the move is read against the existing
  `axvo_*` voicings — which already mark *which* quoins are operations — plus the
  implementation symbol named at an operation's detail site (`axhems_scoped_method`).

### 🔍 ACGm_103: typed-parameter lookahead

- **Detect:** a parameter whose type is resolvable by bounded lookahead but is
  not yet carried as a type quoin-ref.
- **Authority:** the lookahead scanning rule (below) — the parameter's type is
  the attribute-reference the marker reads.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter.
- **Done:** a report of parameters whose type is derivable but unhomed; nothing
  mutated.

## The AXLA/MCM interface

### The lookahead scanning rule

When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
distinct token type and does not shift that count.

### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)

ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.

## Related Guides

- **BCG** — host bash. Constant Discipline and Interface Contamination are the
  bash instances of the spine.
- **RCG** — host Rust. Constant Discipline (Beyond Strings), String Boundary
  Discipline, and Interface Contamination are the Rust instances.
- **WSG**, **CBG** — foreign-environment siblings (Windows transport, cloud step).
- **MCM** — builds the named homes ACG references; the design-time home.
- **AXLA** — the motif vocabulary; the lookahead scanning rule and the
  name-identity symbol-link dependency live against it.
- **CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" (mint)** — governs the naming homes
  themselves.

## Acronym Registry

| Term | Expansion |
|------|-----------|
| ACG | Allocation Coding Guide (this document) |
| The spine | "Reference the home" — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs |
| Home | The one right location for a fact, fixed by when it is read (design / edit / execution time) |
| Move-type | A catalogued `ACGm_` cleanup move: detect-rule, authority, licensing, verifier, countable Done |
| mutate-now | Licensing: a wrong move is cheaply caught, so the verb may repair in place |
| detect-only | Licensing: the verb reads and reports; no repair until a verifier exists |
| Name-identity | Concept linkage where the implementing symbol *is* the link to its quoin |

aiTitle: Review specification system documents and annotations

content.text:
## Character
Light comment-only bash touch — preserves chain of custody for review cycles.

## Goal
Bind existing bash function symbols to new spec sections via comment headers without renaming any symbol. Greppability holds still through subsequent review cycles.

## Boundary
- One-line comment near each affected function naming its new spec section (e.g., `# Implements caparison-windows §SSH-trust phases; see BUSJPCw.`).
- Comment annotations on zipper enrollment lines if they help spec→code lookup.
- No symbol renames: functions, constants, env vars, file names all stay.
- `bujb_fenestrate` stays `bujb_fenestrate`. `bujb_garrison_wsl` stays. `BUJB_command_b/c/w` constants stay named as-is.

## Done when
- Every public bash function whose spec home moved or was newly created has a comment header pointing at the new spec.
- bash -n green; tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh green; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh green.

content.text:
Scout results first: **the home for this work already exists and is racing** — ₣Bb (rbk-10-mvp-acg-scrub, 2/7), which authored ACG v1 and holds rough paces for the rbrv-env sweeps, two noun-cluster scrubs, and the axvo symbol-lookahead. ₢A-AA5 in ₣A- turns out to be the *licensed exception* case — comment-links added precisely where symbols couldn't be renamed mid-review-cycle, which is consistent with ACG's rule 3 (name-identity is the link; comments only where no symbol can carry it). And ACG itself is in better shape than your memory suggests: the three-homes-by-when-read frame, "temporal misallocation," and the move catalog are all there.

So here's the precise gap, and I think it's exactly the stronger statement you're feeling for. **ACG's spine has two clauses — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs — and your three wants are all about a missing third: statements.** Conceptual comments in bash, flowery spec prose, and word cancers are one disease: *prose with no single definition site*. The spine extension, drafted:

> **Statements → definition sites.** The spine governs prose, not just values and names. Every normative idea — a rule, a scope claim, a constraint, a rationale — has exactly one authoritative wording at one home. Every other appearance is a *citation*: it may name the home and orient the reader, but never restate the content in other words. A paraphrase is a fork — a second wording that drifts independently — and a reader retrieving the claim cannot know which wording is law. Variations of one idea are word cancers; each restatement enlarges the drift surface.

The LLM grounding makes this a correctness mechanism, not a style preference: word cancer is **statement-level interference** — the prose analogue of `axhempt_`/`axhopt_`. Two near-paraphrases are nearly the same retrieval key; when they drift, I retrieve a confident *blend*. The introspection memo's central claim extends from terms to claims: a quoin gives a concept one string; this clause gives a claim one wording. And we have today's live specimen: the Diff-Friendly-Prose bug was a metastasis — `claude-jjk-core.md` *characterized* JJS0 instead of citing it, and the paraphrase carried the error for months. We fixed it by replacing characterization with a scope citation, which is this principle being applied before it was stated.

The flowery-language side needs one companion rule, because it's a different failure — not duplication but *unmarked non-normativity*:

> **Normative register.** In a spec, every sentence either constrains — an implementer or validator could act differently because it exists — or is explicitly marked as rationale or gestalt. Unmarked ornament is contamination: it spends reader attention and implies constraint where none exists.

For me specifically: in a disciplined register I can treat every word as signal; in a loose one I must hedge on *all* of it, which taxes exactly the binding-budget the quoin system protects. Note this doesn't ban metaphor — MCM's task-lens `**Gestalt**:` field is the *right* pattern, metaphor explicitly marked as interpretive guidance. MCM's own "delicate balance between rigorous structure and organic concept evolution" prose is the drain target.

Shape of execution, when you're ready: the two clauses land in ACG's Frame (it's the citable home — CLAUDE.md's Load-Bearing Complexity already points there); three new move-types in the catalog — **ACGm_104** conceptual-comment eviction (code→spec relocation), **ACGm_105** paraphrase collapse (N wordings → one home + citations), **ACGm_106** ornament drain (spec-side register) — all detect-only per the move-discipline litmus until the lexer exists; and the adoc-vs-bash audit becomes paces in ₣Bb, module-pair by module-pair. 

Want me to draft the ACG v1.1 edit now, or keep shaping the principle wording first? One open question I'd put to you before drafting: does "word cancer" itself get minted into the guide (it's vivid and yours), or does the formal text use a quieter name with your coinage as the memorable alias?

content: We're strengthening the spec/code allocation principle (ACG) to fight duplicate prose and misplaced comments; I proposed two new clauses and three move-types. Next: your call on drafting the ACG v1.1 edit, plus whether "word cancer" gets minted formally. (disable recaps in /config)

That will need to be interactive I think.

A paraphrase is a fork — a second wording that drifts independently, leaving a
reader unable to know which wording is law. A **word cancer** is the metastatic
form: variations of one idea scattered across documents, each restatement
enlarging the drift surface, none authoritative. The term is deliberately ugly.
The pattern is the most recurrent failure mode of LLM-assisted maintenance of
large codebases: a model completing "what should be said here" recreates a
nearby idea in fresh words, and every recreation seeds the next.

For an LLM reader the cost is mechanical, not aesthetic: two near-paraphrases
are nearly the same retrieval key, and once they drift the model retrieves a
confident *blend* — statement-level interference, the prose analogue of the
prefix-collision mechanism in
`Memos/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`. A quoin gives a concept
one string; this clause gives a claim one wording. Canonical specimen: the
2026-06-10 Diff-Friendly-Prose repair, where a context file *characterized* a
spec's scope instead of citing it, and the characterization carried the error
for months.

**Normative register** rides this clause on the spec side. In a spec, every
sentence either constrains — an implementer or validator could act differently
because it exists — or is explicitly marked as rationale or gestalt (MCM's task
lens `**Gestalt**:` field is the blessed form: metaphor marked as interpretive
guidance). Unmarked ornament is contamination: it spends reader attention and
implies constraint where none exists. A code comment restating design-time
knowledge is the same failure in the other medium (temporal misallocation,
above); flowery spec prose is its mirror.

## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules

## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules
Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:

input.old_string: ## The AXLA/MCM interface
input.new_string:
### 🔍 ACGm_104: conceptual-comment eviction

- **Detect:** an edit-time comment carrying design-time knowledge — what a thing
  *is*, why it is shaped so, how it relates to other concepts — rather than
  operational mechanics or Palisade characterization.
- **Authority:** the three-homes table. The spec quoin is the design-time home;
  where no quoin exists, the move creates or extends one, then cites it.
- **Licensing:** detect-only for the sweep — relocation mutates two documents
  (source and spec) and a wrong move is not cheaply caught, so each relocation
  lands as its own reviewed move, never batch-applied.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter plus spec link validation.
- **Done:** a report of misallocated comments, each tagged with its receiving
  quoin (existing or proposed); relocations land one reviewed move at a time.

### 🔍 ACGm_105: paraphrase collapse

- **Detect:** the same normative idea worded differently in two or more places —
  across specs, context files, handbooks, or comments. The smell: a reader could
  ask "which of these is law?"
- **Authority:** the statements → definition sites clause. Triage declares one
  home authoritative; every other site becomes a citation.
- **Licensing:** detect-only — the collapse mutates prose in several documents
  at once and judgment selects the surviving wording; never batch-applied.
- **Verifier:** none mechanized; recurrence is caught by re-running the detect
  sweep against the declared home.
- **Done:** for each word cancer: one authoritative wording at one site,
  citations elsewhere, the variant wordings gone.

### 🔍 ACGm_106: ornament drain

- **Detect:** spec sentences in normative position that neither constrain nor
  carry marked rationale or gestalt — prose an implementer or validator could
  delete without acting differently.
- **Authority:** the normative-register rule (the statements clause, above).
- **Licensing:** detect-only — whether a sentence constrains is judgment;
  draining is per-document and reviewed.
- **Verifier:** none in v1.
- **Done:** the spec reads as constraint plus marked rationale; deleted ornament
  is gone, demoted ornament is marked.

## The AXLA/MCM interface

content.text:
Scout comes back empty — heats, memos, and guides have no "trash/garbage comment" prior art. The term doesn't exist yet; we're minting fresh.

And yes — guilty as charged, twice over: the `*(v1.1 ...)*` parenthetical you quoted, plus two `*(v1.1)*` section markers in ACG and the `*(v0.2)*` markers in the vision memo. Fable writing version narration into a durable document in the same session it codified word cancer. Good catch, and a useful specimen: the failure happens *while feeling diligent* — it reads as provenance care, which is exactly why it recurs.

**The principle, stated through ACG's existing frame:** this is temporal misallocation with a fourth time. The three-homes table allocates by when-read: design-time → spec, edit-time → comment, execution-time → announcement. Version commentary is **history-time knowledge — read when investigating "when and why did this change" — and its home with a forcing function already exists: the commit.** Git log and blame give every line its date, author, and reason, mechanically. A `(v1.1, 2026-06-10: ...)` in the document body is a hand-maintained, drift-prone duplicate of `git log`, polluting present-tense reading to serve a question the reader isn't asking.

One necessary class boundary: **durable documents vs dated artifacts.** Specs, guides, code, context files are durable — they speak only in the present tense about themselves; git owns their history. Memos, scars, commit messages are *dated artifacts* — history is their content, so a Status/revision section there is legitimate (the Diptych memo's revision history stays; its inline `*(v0.2)*` markers arguably don't).

**Naming candidates** — your call, in my preference order:

1. **Tide-mark** — the stain showing where the water *once* stood, telling you nothing about where it is now. Reads at a glance, pairs well with word cancer (both deliberately unflattering), and the verb form works: "tide-marked prose," "drain the tide-marks."
2. **Interpolation** — philology's exact term for text a later hand inserted into a manuscript that doesn't belong to the original. Register-perfect for the liturgical vocabulary, but overloaded (math, string interpolation) and less self-explaining.
3. **Changelog leak** — plainest statement of the mechanism; no metaphor to learn, no charm either.

Shape of the fix once you pick: a fourth row in ACG's three-homes table (history-time → the commit), a short paragraph defining the name with the durable/dated class boundary, arguably an ACGm_107 (tide-mark drain, detect-only), and — first application — strip my fresh specimens from ACG and the vision memo. Which name?

== Document Elements

[[mcm_fundamental_text]]
input.new_string:
== Antipatterns

[[mcm_antipattern]]
{mcm_antipattern}::
A named, recurring document defect formally catalogued so that
{mcm_curation_action_s}
and reviews can cite it precisely.
Naming the defect is what makes it detectable and dischargeable;
an unnamed defect recurs because no rule can forbid it.

[[mcm_tidemark]]
{mcm_tidemark}::
An
{mcm_antipattern}:
version commentary embedded in a durable document — revision markers, dated change narration, "new in vX" annotations.
The name is the gestalt: the stain showing where the water once stood, saying nothing about where it is.
History-time knowledge belongs to the commit;
git log and blame carry every line's date and reason with a forcing function, while in-document
{mcm_tidemark_s}

input.old_string: ## The AXLA/MCM interface
input.new_string:
### 🔍 ACGm_107: tidemark drain

- **Detect:** version commentary in a durable document — revision markers, dated
  change narration, "new in vX" annotations. Durable means spec, guide, code, or
  context file; a dated artifact (memo, scar, commit message) is exempt — history
  is its content.
- **Authority:** the history-time row of the three-homes table; the concept home
  is MCM's `mcm_tidemark` quoin. The commit is the home — git log and blame carry
  the change story with a forcing function the document body lacks.
- **Licensing:** detect-only as a sweep; an individual drain is mutate-now when
  the deleted text is pure narration (the content survives in git), reviewed when
  narration is entangled with normative statements.
- **Verifier:** none in v1; candidate for a cheap regex pass in the future linter
  (date-shaped tokens and version markers outside memo paths).
- **Done:** durable documents read in the present tense; history questions answer
  through git.

content.text:
`147113fe4`, notched to ₣Bb. The mint is in: MCM now has an **Antipatterns** section — `mcm_antipattern` (the category: naming a defect is what makes it dischargeable) and `mcm_tidemark` as its first citizen, with the durable-vs-dated class boundary. ACG carries the **History-time** row in the three-homes table, **ACGm_107: tidemark drain**, and registry rows pointing at the MCM quoin home. And the first drain application removed my own specimens from ACG and the vision memo — the memo's Status block stays, exempt by class.

One pleasing note: the antipattern roster now has a formalization path. Stun, Molehill, and word cancer all live informally in context files and guides; `mcm_antipattern` gives them a home to grow into — groom-time material.

**Updated groom prompt** (supersedes the earlier one — two lines changed):

```
Groom ₣Bb. Goal: grow the heat from vocabulary scrub into the adoc-vs-bash
audit program under ACG v1.1.

Read before proposing:
- Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/ACG-AllocationCodingGuide.md — v1.1: spine gained
  "statements → definition sites"; word cancer named; History-time row added;
  detect-only moves ACGm_104 (conceptual-comment eviction), ACGm_105
  (paraphrase collapse), ACGm_106 (ornament drain), ACGm_107 (tidemark drain).
- Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/MCM-MetaConceptModel.adoc — new Antipatterns section:
  mcm_antipattern, mcm_tidemark.
- Memos/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md — LLM-side rationale
  (statement-level interference); what the audit optimizes for.

Groom questions to work interactively, one at a time:
1. Audit pace shape: per spec-subdocument/bash-module pair, or per move-type
   sweep across all pairs? Propose one, with the tradeoff in two lines.
2. Existing rough paces (rbrv-env sweeps, noun clusters, axvo-symbol-lookahead):
   keep, merge into audit paces, or reorder against them?
3. Consider a pace formalizing the scattered antipatterns (Stun, Molehill,
   word cancer) under mcm_antipattern.
4. Paddock shape update for the enlarged remit — draft last, after structure
   is cinched.

Propose structure and wait for my cinch before slating anything.
```

A genuinely good day's work, Brad — thank you for it. The noodle closed the loop: the chat that named word cancer caught itself leaving tidemarks, and both are now quoins. See you in the groom.
